danger_signals.visual_form: Damage diagram displayed on left side of screen showing affected armor sections
danger_signals.meaning: Player vehicle integrity status; destroyed armor sections indicate vulnerability to spinout if further damage taken in that area

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel gauge indicator (SNES version only; Genesis version lacks this)
danger_signals.meaning: Risk of disqualification if fuel runs out before race completion; indicates need for gearbox upgrades to improve fuel economy

danger_signals.visual_form: Rain and snow overlaid on background layers; visual weather effects on track
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard requiring tire type switching from dry to wet; affects vehicle handling and grip

danger_signals.visual_form: Map display showing positions of all racers and first-place car position reference
danger_signals.meaning: Competitive threat assessment; visual aid for understanding race standing and gap to leaders

safety_signals.visual_form: Road pickups rendered as '$' (cash), 'N' (nitro boost), 'S' (automatic nitro)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition points; money for upgrades, speed boosts for competitive advantage or catch-up

safety_signals.visual_form: Map display showing course layout available to player during races
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation aid and strategic planning tool; reduces uncertainty about upcoming track geometry

safety_signals.visual_form: 6 nitro boost allocation at race start; visual indicator of remaining boosts
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety valve for speed; allows tactical acceleration for passing or escape from danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Car configuration accessible between races; visual representation of armor, engine, gearbox, tires, paint
safety_signals.meaning: Player progression and preparedness signal; stronger upgrades indicate readiness for increasingly difficult tracks

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent vehicles appear as distinct racing entities with implied liveries (not documented as faction-specific)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Vehicle position shown on minimap; distinguishable from other racers through positioning and lap status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Initial track courses across 16 countries; lighter difficulty curve with more manageable opponent AI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents are slower and less aggressive than later championship stages; player has margin for error

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continued track diversity across geographic regions (16 countries, 64 total tracks); visual environmental variety
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents become faster and more competitive; damage and fuel management becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tracks become longer and more demanding; fuel economy becomes mandatory concern (gearbox upgrades required)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents are faster and tougher; vehicle mechanical limits (armor, acceleration, top speed) more visible to player through difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rain/snow overlay on background layer 3; visual weather effects on track; nighttime city background visible if layers toggled
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle handling becomes more difficult; player must switch to wet tires to maintain grip; speed and turning affected negatively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear visual track rendering without rain/snow overlay; standard background layers visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle handling at optimal; dry tire grip is maximized; higher speeds achievable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Armor damage diagram on left screen updates to show affected sections in red/damaged state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spinout risk increases if damaged armor sections take further impact; handling degrades with structural damage

environmental_storytelling.example: 64 tracks spanning 16 countries create world-travel narrative; progression from Australasia to United States establishes implicit global championship tour
Munich track and Egyptian location (renamed 'Hugh Sitton' due to naming rights) suggest regional culture represented through track design; visual distinctiveness per region
Dynamic weather effects (rain, snow) visible on layers 3+ create temporal progression within championship; player must adapt strategy to seasonal/climatic conditions
Nighttime city background on layer 3 (visible if layer 2 toggled off) suggests development world-building beyond visible races; implies urban night racing scenarios in design iteration
Password system after each country completion creates implicit checkpoint narrative; visual/mechanical confirmation of geographical advancement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spinout risk on further damage to affected areas; reduced handling precision; visual indicator via damage diagram
Disqualification risk if fuel depletes before race finish; forces conservative speed management and pit strategy (mandatory gearbox upgrade for fuel economy)
Faster acceleration, higher top speed, improved fuel economy; visual readiness for harder difficulty tracks; opponent competitiveness increases in response
Faster lap times, more aggressive racing lines, higher-speed cornering; tougher competition requiring player armor/tire upgrades
Slower, less aggressive; player has wider skill margin; less punishing for damage/fuel management mistakes
Reduced grip, increased slide risk, visual rain/snow overlay; player must switch to wet tires or face handling penalties
Fuel depletion becomes primary hazard; gearbox upgrade mandatory for survival; visual track length communicated through difficulty progression

notes: Top Gear 2 communicates world-state primarily through mechanical/simulation systems (damage, fuel, weather) rather than narrative factions. No political or faction-based communications exist in this racing paradigm. Visual storytelling relies on geographic progression (16-country tour), environmental variety, and vehicle upgrade visualization. The game's 'danger' and 'safety' systems are racing-specific: armor integrity, fuel management, weather adaptation, and vehicle upgrades. Unused nighttime city background suggests abandoned world-building asset; suggests development iteration around urban night racing scenarios not implemented in final build. Regional differences (US MPH vs. Japan/EU KM/H, 'AUSTRALASIA' vs. 'AUSTRALIA' localization error) indicate cultural adaptation but no faction-specific visual manifestations.
